title: Ichtyoselmis macrantha
taxonomy: Ichtyoselmis macrantha belongs to the class Chlorophyceae, which is a division of green algae. This classification places it within the broader kingdom of Chlorophyta. Within the class Chlorophyceae, Ichtyoselmis macrantha is further classified as a member of the order Tetrasporales. The family to which it belongs is Tetrasporaceae, and it is one of several genera within this family. The exact genus classification of Ichtyoselmis macrantha is a bit more nuanced and may require further research.

intro: Icthyoselmis macrantha, also known as Australian watermeal, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the Lemnaceae family, consisting of small, flat, and oval-shaped leaves that grow in clusters on the water surface. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Icthyoselmis macrantha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
